avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Shallow accumulations of fresh unstable windslab will form in steep wind sheltered locations on mainly South-West to North-West aspects above 850 metres. These localised deposits will be shallow and generally not significant. Areas most affected will be coire rims, gully tops and steep banks and hollows. Elsewhere the snowpack will continue to  consolidate and will be firm and generally stable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Mainly dry overnight with the freezing level briefly rising to around the highest summits. Light showers through the morning falling as snow above around 600 metres. Fresh to strong Easterly winds around dawn will increase through the day.
Observed Weather Influences: A generally dry and cloudy day with light showers falling as snow above 500 metres. Feeling very cold in moderate to fresh South-Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised and shallow areas of soft windslab have formed in wind sheltered locations on mainly West to North aspects above 850 metres. Amounts are generally not significant though some deeper drifts exist. Fresh cornices have also formed in steeper locations on these aspects. An existing layer of more wide spread, firm and reasonably bonded windslab remains on West to North aspects above 850 metres which is generally stable. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Good stability on all windward slopes where there will be hard or crusty conditions. Older windslab on recent lee slopes expected to consolidate over the forecast period. New weak localised windslab development anticipated on steep sheltered West to North-West aspects above 950m; some selected steep North facing slopes and gullies will also be affected. Cornices will overhang some of these locations.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A period of snowfall overnight and into the morning then generally dry. Strong East-South-Easterly winds throughout. Briefly freezing near summits overnight then progressively colder again.
Observed Weather Influences: Persistent light snowfall from the early hours onwards. Strong East-South-Easterly airflow. Cold throughout.
ObservedSnowStability: Most windward slopes remain scoured down to hard or crusty snow ice and here stability is good. Persistent drifting resulted in new weak windslab build up mainly on steep West to North-West aspects above 950m; selected steep North aspects also affected. New weak cornice development above steepest lee slopes.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Little change is expected.  Some areas of dense and reasonable well bonded windslab are expected mainly on sheltered West to North aspects above 800 metres. These are not expected to be extensive, and should be generally avoidable. Elsewhere the snowpack will be well consolidated and generally stable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will rise to around 1000 metres overnight before falling to around 500 metres through the day. The gale force East to South-East winds will continue. The odd light shower is possible, but otherwise it will stay mainly dry.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a cold day with gale force South-Easterly winds. The freezing level was around 600 metres. There was some light snow showers.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is generally firm and stable with a firm icy crust in many places. There are a few mainly shallow and well bonded areas of fresh windslab in sheltered West to North aspects above 800 metres. These are generally avoidable. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Windslab will continue to develop with avalanches likely on West to North aspects above 800 metres. The greatest instability will be in steep locations such as gullies, corrie head walls and convexities. Cross loading will affect South-West and North-East aspects. Fragile cornices will continue to develop on the same aspects. Between 500 and 800 meters, moderately bonded windslab will continue to develop on steep banks, convexities and burn lines.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snowfall from the middle of the night onwards on gale force South-East becoming East-South-Easterly winds. During the night the freezing level will rise to 900 metres for a few hours before gradually falling to 500 metres by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale force South-Easterly winds with snowfall and significant drifting above 500 metres.  The freezing level was around 600 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Accumulations of windslab continued to build on West to North aspects above 500m. South-West and North-East aspects were affected by cross loading. The greatest instability is above 800 metres in steep wind sheltered locations such as corrie back walls, gullies and crag aprons. Fragile cornices continue to build above these aspects. On these aspects between 500 and 800 metres moderately bonded windslab is present on steep banks, burn lines and convexities. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Moderately to weakly bonded windslab will continue to form in wind sheltered locations on West to North aspects above 900 metres with avalanches likely. Windslab will also develop below this altitude as the freezing level lowers during the day. Cross-loaded deposits will be present on South-West and North-East aspects. Fragile cornices will form. Slopes exposed to the wind will be scoured and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snowfall arriving late evening, initially above 900 metres, eventually falling as snow to around 500 metres. Strong South-Easterly winds overnight, backing East-South-Easterly by daylight. Freezing level rising to 900 metres for a couple of hours around midnight, lowering to 500 metres by the end of the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: Overnight snowfall down to 350 metres, eased to light snow showers during the day. Gale force East-South-Easterly winds, eased slightly in the afternoon. Freezing level around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Snowfall and constant drifting of deposits have formed moderately to weakly bonded windslab in wind sheltered locations on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres. Moderately bonded snow is widespread below this altitude and above 700 metres. Fragile cornices have formed. Slopes exposed to the winds are scoured and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will be generally firm and stable. Isolated deposits of windslab present on South-West, West and North-West aspects above 800 metres will be consolidating. Any fresh hill snow is not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Very strong Easterly winds will steadily increase to become gale force. The freezing level will go above the summits for a brief period overnight and then decrease to around 500 metres. There may be light hill snow but this is not expected to be significant.
Observed Weather Influences: Very strong Easterly winds with the freezing level around 450 metres. Snow showers over inland ranges, drier towards coastal mountains.
ObservedSnowStability: Over inland mountain ranges there is a dusting of fresh snow, where isolated windslab deposits are developing on South-West, West and North-West aspects above 800 metres. Elsewhere the snowpack is firm and stable.The avalanche hazard is Low.
